title: The Economic, Social and Cultural Council Registration 2026–2027: How African Civil Society Organizations Can Gain African Union Recognition and Participate in Continental Policy Dialogue
summary: The African Union’s Economic, Social and Cultural Council (ECOSOCC) has officially opened registration for organizations seeking to join the ECOSOCC Connect platform, a major continental initiative designed to strengthen the participation of African civil society and diaspora organizations in African Union policymaking and development processes. Through ECOSOCC Connect, organizations across Africa and the diaspora can create official profiles, apply for accreditation, participate in African Union consultations, and contribute directly to continental dialogue on governance, development, social transformation, and public policy. The platform serves as the recognized gateway for African civil society organizations to engage with the African Union and become part of a continent-wide network shaping Africa’s future. In addition to the ongoing registration process, ECOSOCC has also announced applications for the 5th Permanent General Assembly (5PGA), a key programme aligned with the ECOSOCC Statute and official African Union participation requirements. ECOSOCC Connect Registration and 5PGA Timeline Registration Status Open for applications and profile creation 5th Permanent General Assembly (5PGA) Opening Date: April 28, 2026 Closing Date: October 29, 2027 Organizations interested in participating in the ECOSOCC General Assembly and broader AU policy activities are encouraged to complete registration early. What Is ECOSOCC? ECOSOCC, short for the Economic, Social and Cultural Council, is an official organ of the African Union created to facilitate the participation of African civil society in the work of the African Union. The platform acts as the institutional bridge between: African citizens Civil society organizations Policymakers African Union institutions Member states ECOSOCC ensures that African civil society has a voice in continental decision-making and development strategies. What Is ECOSOCC Connect? ECOSOCC Connect is the African Union’s digital platform designed for: Civil society registration Accreditation management Policy participation Continental networking Organizational visibility The platform allows eligible organizations to: Create official profiles Register organizations online Track accreditation processes Access African Union opportunities Participate in consultations and programmes The initiative represents a major step toward improving transparency, accessibility, and inclusion in African Union civic engagement processes. Why ECOSOCC Connect Matters for African Civil Society Civil society organizations play a critical role in advancing democracy, human rights, youth empowerment, development, gender equality, education, public health, and governance across Africa. However, many organizations face barriers when attempting to engage directly with continental institutions and policymaking spaces. ECOSOCC Connect addresses this challenge by creating a centralized and officially recognized platform through which organizations can interact with the African Union. The platform gives organizations: Visibility Recognition Participation opportunities Policy engagement channels Access to continental networks Key Benefits of Registering on ECOSOCC Connect Organizations that register on the platform gain multiple strategic advantages. 1. Continental Recognition Registered organizations receive formal recognition within the African Union ecosystem. This recognition strengthens: Organizational credibility Institutional legitimacy Regional visibility Partnership opportunities It also demonstrates that the organization is actively engaged in continental development and civic participation. 2. Participation in African Union Activities Accredited organizations can apply to participate in: Consultations Policy dialogues Programmes AU-led initiatives Continental events Development discussions This creates direct opportunities for organizations to influence policymaking processes at the continental level. 3. Inclusion in the Official Continental CSO Database Registered organizations are listed in the official African Union civil society database. This improves visibility among: African Union organs AU member states Development agencies Regional institutions Potential collaborators and partners 4. Streamlined Online Accreditation ECOSOCC Connect modernizes and simplifies the accreditation process. Organizations can: Submit applications online Upload required documents digitally Track application progress Receive guided submission support This removes many administrative barriers traditionally associated with accreditation processes. 5. Voice in African Union Policy Processes One of the most important benefits of ECOSOCC accreditation is policy engagement. Organizations can contribute expertise and recommendations through: Sectoral Cluster Committees Policy consultations Continental discussions Advocacy platforms This ensures that civil society perspectives are integrated into African Union decision-making processes. Who Can Register on ECOSOCC Connect? The platform is open to a broad range of African civil society and diaspora organizations. Eligible Organizations Include: 1. African Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) Organizations operating within African Union member states can apply for registration and accreditation. Examples include: Nonprofits Advocacy organizations Community groups NGOs Research organizations Youth organizations Women-led organizations Human rights groups 2. African Diaspora Organizations Organizations representing African diaspora communities worldwide are also eligible. This allows Africans living outside the continent to participate in AU policy conversations and development initiatives. 3. Organizations Operating in AU Member States Eligible organizations must be: Registered Operational Active within one or more of the 55 African Union member states or the diaspora African Union Member States Covered ECOSOCC Connect serves organizations across all 55 African Union member states, including: Algeria Angola Benin Botswana Burkina Faso Burundi Cabo Verde Cameroon Central African Republic Chad Comoros Congo Democratic Republic of Congo Côte d’Ivoire Djibouti Egypt Equatorial Guinea Eritrea Eswatini Ethiopia Gabon Gambia Ghana Guinea Guinea-Bissau Kenya Lesotho Liberia Libya Madagascar Malawi Mali Mauritania Mauritius Morocco Mozambique Namibia Niger Nigeria Rwanda Sahrawi Republic São Tomé and Príncipe Senegal Seychelles Sierra Leone Somalia South Africa South Sudan Sudan Tanzania Togo Tunisia Uganda Zambia Zimbabwe About the 5th Permanent General Assembly (5PGA) One of the major opportunities currently available through ECOSOCC Connect is participation in the 5th Permanent General Assembly (5PGA). The 5PGA serves as: A continental civil society engagement platform A governance and representation mechanism A framework for structured AU-CSO collaboration The application cycle is aligned with: The ECOSOCC Statute Official application requirements AU governance standards Organizations interested in deeper continental participation are encouraged to apply. Languages Available on the Platform To ensure inclusivity and accessibility across Africa and the diaspora, ECOSOCC Connect is available in multiple languages, including: English French Arabic Kiswahili Portuguese Spanish This multilingual approach helps organizations from diverse regions participate more effectively. Frequently Asked Questions About ECOSOCC Connect Does My Organization Need to Be Based in Africa? No. African diaspora organizations outside the continent are also eligible. Can Organizations Less Than Three Years Old Apply? The platform includes eligibility guidance regarding organizational maturity and accreditation requirements. Applicants are encouraged to review the official criteria carefully. Can Applications Be Edited After Submission? The platform includes online tracking and guided submission systems that support organizations throughout the process. What Documents Are Required? Organizations will typically need: Registration certificates Organizational profiles Governance documents Supporting institutional information Applicants should consult the official ECOSOCC guidelines for complete requirements. Why Organizations Should Join ECOSOCC Connect Joining ECOSOCC Connect provides organizations with: Continental visibility African Union recognition Access to policy discussions Networking opportunities Governance participation Advocacy platforms Institutional credibility For organizations committed to shaping Africa’s future, ECOSOCC Connect offers a direct pathway into continental dialogue and decision-making. Official Registration and Application Links Organizations can register and apply through the official ECOSOCC Connect platform. Official Website: ECOSOCC Connect Platform Create Profile: ECOSOCC Registration Portal Discover more global membership opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post The Economic, Social and Cultural Council Registration 2026–2027: How African Civil Society Organizations Can Gain African Union Recognition and Participate in Continental Policy Dialogue appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: William T. Grant Foundation Institutional Challenge Grant 2026: Up to $650,000 to Strengthen Research-Practice Partnerships and Reduce Youth Inequality in the United States
summary: The William T. Grant Foundation has officially launched applications for its highly competitive 2026 Institutional Challenge Grant, a transformative funding opportunity aimed at helping research institutions and public-serving organizations build long-term partnerships that improve youth outcomes across the United States. This grant program supports university-based research institutes, schools, and centers that collaborate with public agencies or nonprofit organizations to address inequality affecting young people aged 5 to 25. Through this initiative, the Foundation seeks to strengthen research-practice partnerships that generate actionable evidence capable of influencing policy, practice, and community outcomes. With grants worth up to $650,000 over three years, the Institutional Challenge Grant is one of the leading funding opportunities in the United States for institutions seeking to bridge the gap between academic research and real-world policymaking. About the William T. Grant Foundation Institutional Challenge Grant The Institutional Challenge Grant was designed to address a major challenge in the research ecosystem: the disconnect between academic research and practical policy implementation. Although universities produce rigorous studies, many research findings fail to influence public systems because they are often developed primarily for academic audiences instead of policymakers and practitioners. As a result, valuable research frequently remains underutilized in solving urgent social problems affecting youth. The William T. Grant Foundation believes that sustainable research-practice partnerships are essential to solving this challenge. Research-practice partnerships are long-term collaborations between researchers and practitioners that promote the production and use of rigorous, relevant, and actionable research evidence. These partnerships allow researchers to better understand community realities while helping agencies and nonprofits apply evidence more effectively in decision-making. The Institutional Challenge Grant specifically supports partnerships that seek to reduce inequality in youth outcomes while simultaneously transforming how institutions value collaborative research. Purpose of the Grant Program The core mission of the Institutional Challenge Grant is to help institutions create sustainable systems that support evidence-based policymaking and youth development. The program encourages research institutions to: Redesign internal policies and incentives Value collaborative and applied research Support long-term partnerships with practitioners Strengthen researchers’ ability to work with agencies and communities Improve agencies’ ability to interpret and use research findings The grant recognizes that effective partnerships require significant investments in trust-building, institutional infrastructure, and long-term collaboration. Focus Areas Supported by the Grant The Foundation welcomes proposals from partnerships working in multiple youth-serving sectors. These include: Education Juvenile justice Child welfare Foster care Mental health Immigration Workforce development Prevention of child abuse and neglect Projects should focus on reducing inequality among youth aged 5–25 in the United States. Types of Inequality the Grant Aims to Address The research agenda supported by the grant should focus on inequalities related to: Race Ethnicity Economic standing Language minority status Immigrant origins Sexual orientation and gender identity LGBTQ+ youth experiences The Foundation strongly encourages proposals that center underserved and historically marginalized populations. Why Research-Practice Partnerships Matter The Foundation emphasizes that research-practice partnerships can significantly improve how institutions and agencies respond to youth challenges. These partnerships help: Researchers understand local realities and policy environments Agencies gain access to rigorous evidence Policymakers interpret and apply research findings Communities benefit from evidence-informed interventions Institutions create sustainable systems for collaboration By combining academic expertise with practitioner experience, partnerships can produce more impactful and actionable solutions. Program Goals of the Institutional Challenge Grant The grant program requires applicants to pursue four major objectives. 1. Expand and Strengthen Existing Partnerships Applicants must demonstrate an existing collaboration between a university-based research institution and a public agency or nonprofit organization. The partnership should include: Shared objectives Clearly defined leadership roles Long-term collaboration plans Joint research priorities Sustainable engagement structures The Foundation prioritizes partnerships that already have some operational history and trust established. 2. Develop a Joint Research Agenda to Reduce Youth Inequality The proposed research agenda must focus on improving academic, social, behavioral, or economic outcomes for young people. Research activities may include: Testing policies and interventions Evaluating programs Identifying barriers to equity Developing evidence-based strategies Improving understanding of systemic inequalities The Foundation expects the research to generate findings that directly influence decision-making and practice. 3. Transform Institutional Policies and Incentives One of the most innovative aspects of this grant is its focus on institutional transformation. Research institutions are expected to examine and redesign policies that discourage collaborative work. Examples may include: Faculty evaluation systems Promotion and tenure policies Funding structures Research incentives Administrative support systems The goal is to create environments where partnership-based research is respected, rewarded, and sustained. 4. Build Capacity for Both Researchers and Practitioners The program seeks to strengthen both sides of the partnership. For researchers, the grant supports: Partnership-building skills Policy engagement Communication skills Applied research methods Understanding of local systems For agencies and nonprofits, the grant supports: Research literacy Data use systems Evidence integration Staff development Technical assistance Funding Amount and Financial Support The Foundation will award at least two Institutional Challenge Grants during the 2026 cycle. Each grant provides up to $650,000 over three years. What the Funding Covers The grant includes: Planning Activities Up to $60,000 for up to nine months of joint planning Partnership development activities Data-sharing agreements Fellow selection processes Partnership protocol development Fellowship Support Two years of funding for a full-time equivalent mid-career fellow Universities must provide funding for one additional year Research Activities Support for collaborative research projects Evidence generation and utilization Data collection and analysis Institutional Capacity Building Resources to support institutional reforms Infrastructure development Training and technical support Indirect Costs Up to 15% indirect cost allowance Opportunity for Continuation Funding After the initial three-year grant period, grantees may apply for an additional two-year continuation grant. This continuation funding helps partnerships: Strengthen institutional reforms Sustain collaborative systems Expand research impact Improve long-term youth outcomes Fellowship Structure and Requirements A major component of the Institutional Challenge Grant is the mid-career fellowship program. Fellowship Options Institutions may structure fellowships in different ways, including: One fellow over three years Multiple fellows over shorter periods Half-time fellowships Shared fellowship structures Mid-Career Eligibility Criteria For researchers: Terminal degree earned within the past 8–20 years For agency professionals: 8–20 years of experience in their current role All fellows must commit to: At least half-time participation A minimum six-month appointment Eligibility Requirements Eligible Research Institutions The Foundation only funds tax-exempt organizations. Eligible applicants include: University-based research institutes Academic schools University centers Not eligible: Independent think tanks Private research organizations Individual applicants Eligible Public Agencies and Nonprofits Eligible partners include: State agencies Local government agencies Nonprofit youth-serving organizations Organizations must provide or coordinate services for youth in the United States. Application Deadline Opening Date: May 15, 2026 Next Application Deadline: September 9, 2026, at 3:00 PM EST Applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their submissions early due to the extensive institutional collaboration and planning required. Institutions Especially Encouraged to Apply The Foundation encourages applications from underrepresented institutions, including: Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) Hispanic-Serving Institutions (HSIs) Tribal Colleges and Universities Alaska Native Serving Institutions Native Hawaiian-Serving Institutions Asian American Native American Pacific Islander Serving Institutions Review Criteria for Applications Applications will be reviewed based on several major criteria. Partnership Strength Reviewers will assess: Existing collaboration quality Trust between partners Sustainability potential Commitment from both institutions Research Quality Applications should demonstrate: Rigorous methodology Clear hypotheses Strong data plans Feasible timelines Practical relevance Institutional Change Strategy Strong applications should: Identify institutional barriers Propose realistic reforms Demonstrate leadership commitment Include sustainability plans Capacity Building Reviewers will examine: Training strategies Technical assistance plans Fellowship quality Research-use systems Expected Outcomes of the Grant Program By the end of the funding cycle, successful grantees are expected to demonstrate: Stronger research-practice partnerships Increased use of research evidence Improved institutional policies Better collaboration between researchers and practitioners Enhanced youth outcomes Sustainable systems for evidence-based decision-making Supporting Foundations and Organizations Since 2018, the Institutional Challenge Grant has been supported by several prominent organizations, including: Spencer Foundation Doris Duke Foundation American Institutes for Research Annie E. Casey Foundation Bezos Family Foundation Their support has enabled the Foundation to increase the number of awards distributed annually. Why This Grant Is a Major Opportunity The Institutional Challenge Grant is more than a traditional research grant. It is an opportunity for institutions to: Transform how research impacts society Build sustainable collaborations Influence public policy Improve youth outcomes Advance equity and inclusion Strengthen institutional capacity For universities and agencies committed to reducing inequality among youth, this grant offers both substantial funding and long-term strategic value. Official Application Link Interested applicants can learn more and access the official application materials through the William T. Grant Foundation website. Read application guide and apply: Click here For more information, visit the official website Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post William T. Grant Foundation Institutional Challenge Grant 2026: Up to $650,000 to Strengthen Research-Practice Partnerships and Reduce Youth Inequality in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Google Semarak Malaysia Fund 2026 in Selangor: Up to RM30,000 Funding for Community Projects
summary: Google is strengthening its commitment to community development in Malaysia through the Google Semarak Malaysia Fund: Selangor, an initiative designed to empower grassroots changemakers, social innovators, schools, cooperatives, and organisations working to improve lives across the State of Selangor. The fund is focused on supporting impactful community-led projects that address pressing social, environmental, educational, and economic challenges. Through this initiative, eligible projects can receive up to RM30,000 in funding, alongside access to expert guidance, practical implementation support, and resources that can help transform innovative ideas into sustainable community impact. This initiative reflects Google’s broader commitment to building stronger communities by investing in workforce development, digital inclusion, environmental sustainability, and small business empowerment. Whether you are a school introducing digital skills programs, a social enterprise helping local businesses grow, or an organisation working on sustainability initiatives, this opportunity provides a pathway to scale meaningful solutions within Selangor. What is the Google Semarak Malaysia Fund? The Google Semarak Malaysia Fund is a community-focused funding initiative aimed at supporting practical projects that can create measurable positive impact in Selangor. The fund seeks to empower local leaders and organisations that are actively contributing to social progress and sustainable development. The initiative is not only about funding. Successful applicants also gain access to: Expert guides and implementation resources Structured step-by-step support Advisory support from the ChangeX team Opportunities to implement proven community ideas A network of changemakers and social innovators The program encourages both new and existing community-driven initiatives that align with specific focus areas identified by the fund. Funding Amount Selected projects can receive funding of up to RM30,000 per project. The exact amount awarded depends on the scope, needs, and impact potential of the proposed initiative. Funding is allocated after the application deadline once all applications have been reviewed and evaluated. Key Focus Areas Projects applying for the Google Semarak Malaysia Fund must align with at least one of the following themes: 1. Workforce Skilling and Career Pathways Projects that equip individuals with employability skills, vocational training, career development opportunities, and future-ready capabilities are encouraged. 2. Supporting Small Businesses Initiatives that help local entrepreneurs and small businesses grow, innovate, and become more resilient are highly encouraged under this funding stream. 3. AI and Digital Training Programs focused on digital literacy, AI education, technology skills, coding, and digital readiness for students and communities are eligible. 4. Watershed and Water Resilience Environmental projects aimed at water sustainability, watershed protection, conservation, and resilience-building can apply. 5. Energy and Sustainability Initiatives Projects designed to reduce environmental footprints, improve energy efficiency, and promote sustainable practices are welcome. Who Can Apply? The Google Semarak Malaysia Fund is open to a broad range of community-focused entities operating in Selangor. Eligible applicants include: Schools ROS-registered societies SSM-registered entities Social enterprises Registered cooperatives Informal organisations and community groups All applicants must demonstrate: A clear social mission Community benefit Proper use of grant funding for impact-driven activities Capacity to complete the proposed project within 12 months Applicants must also be at least 18 years old and authorised representatives of their organisations. Project Location Requirement All funded projects must take place within the State of Selangor, Malaysia. This location requirement ensures that the initiative directly benefits local communities and contributes to sustainable regional development. How the Application Process Works The application process has been designed to be accessible and straightforward. Step 1: Submit Your Application Applications must be submitted by June 30, 2026 (+08). Applicants can either: Apply with their own original project idea, or Apply to implement a proven idea already available through the ChangeX platform Step 2: Review Process All applications will be reviewed according to: Eligibility criteria Community impact potential Alignment with fund themes Feasibility and sustainability Successful applicants will be notified by August 11, 2026 (+08). Step 3: Complete Onboarding Requirements Selected applicants must complete simple onboarding steps within 30 days before project implementation begins. Step 4: Receive Funding Once onboarding is completed, the first portion of funding is disbursed to support project implementation. Step 5: Submit Impact Report Grant recipients must provide both qualitative and quantitative impact data within 12 months of receiving funding. Impact reporting is necessary to unlock the final portion of the funding. What the Funding Can Be Used For Funding may cover: Project setup costs Community space rental Insurance fees Tools and materials Seeds and planting materials Training costs Maintenance costs Operational support Payments for project workers in some cases What the Funding Cannot Be Used For Funding cannot be used for: Previously completed work Activities unrelated to the project Religious promotion Fundraising events Sponsorships Criminal or violent activities Hate speech or discriminatory activities Important Funding Conditions Applicants should note the following: Individuals and informal groups can receive funding for one project at a time Registered organisations can manage up to three projects simultaneously if each is led by different team members Previous recipients who failed to submit impact reports may become ineligible for future funding Why You Should Apply The Google Semarak Malaysia Fund offers more than financial support. It provides communities with the opportunity to transform innovative ideas into scalable, sustainable impact. For organisations seeking to improve digital literacy, empower youth, strengthen local businesses, or advance sustainability goals, this initiative provides both the funding and support system needed to succeed. By investing in grassroots leadership and community innovation, Google is helping build a more inclusive and resilient future for Selangor. Deadline June 30, 2026 (+08) APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here For more global opportunities, click here The post Google Semarak Malaysia Fund 2026 in Selangor: Up to RM30,000 Funding for Community Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: The Barnes Firm Law School Scholarship 2026 in the USA | $5,000 Tuition Support for Law Students
summary: Higher education remains one of the most powerful tools for shaping future leaders, advocates, and professionals who can make meaningful contributions to society. Recognizing the importance of supporting aspiring legal professionals,The Barnes Firm has officially announced the 2026 edition of its prestigious Law School Scholarship program. Led by Rich Barnes, President of The Barnes Firm, this scholarship initiative is designed to assist dedicated law students in pursuing their academic and professional goals. The program reflects the organization’s commitment to fostering educational advancement, leadership development, and community engagement among future legal professionals in the United States. The Barnes Firm Law School Scholarship will award financial assistance to two deserving students enrolled in accredited law schools for the Fall 2026 academic year. Each selected recipient will receive a scholarship award of $5,000 to support tuition expenses. This opportunity is particularly beneficial for students who have demonstrated academic excellence, leadership potential, and active involvement within their communities. About The Barnes Firm Law School Scholarship The Barnes Firm is widely recognized for its work in personal injury law across California and New York State. Through this scholarship program, the organization aims to invest in the next generation of legal professionals who are passionate about making a positive difference in society. According to the announcement, the scholarship was established to support talented students who aspire to become impactful leaders and advocates in their respective communities. The initiative also reinforces the organization’s broader mission of empowering individuals through access to education and professional development opportunities. By providing financial support, The Barnes Firm hopes to ease some of the economic burdens associated with legal education while encouraging students to continue striving for excellence both academically and socially. Scholarship Benefits The Barnes Firm Law School Scholarship offers substantial support for selected recipients. Key benefits include: Two students will be selected as scholarship recipients Each recipient will receive $5,000 Funds will go toward tuition at an accredited law school The scholarship applies to the Fall 2026 academic enrollment period Recognition from a nationally recognized personal injury law firm The scholarship not only provides financial relief but also serves as an acknowledgment of students’ hard work, leadership qualities, and dedication to community service. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for The Barnes Firm Law School Scholarship, applicants must meet the following requirements: Applicants must be United States citizens Students must either be accepted into or currently enrolled in an accredited law school Applicants must maintain a cumulative GPA of 3.0 or higher Students must demonstrate involvement in community activities Applicants must complete and submit the official online application form In addition, candidates are encouraged to follow The Barnes Firm on social media to stay informed about scholarship updates and winner announcements. Selection Criteria Scholarship recipients will be selected based on several important factors that highlight both academic and personal achievements. The selection committee will evaluate applicants using the following criteria: Academic Achievement Applicants with strong academic performance and consistent educational excellence will receive favorable consideration. Community Involvement The scholarship strongly emphasizes civic responsibility and active participation in community-based activities. Students who demonstrate leadership and service are encouraged to apply. Essay Submission Applicants will also be required to submit an essay as part of the application process. The essay provides candidates with an opportunity to showcase their goals, experiences, motivations, and commitment to making a meaningful impact through the legal profession. The final decisions regarding scholarship recipients will be made by The Barnes Firm College Scholarship Committee, and all decisions are considered final and binding. Important Scholarship Deadlines Students interested in applying should take note of the official timeline for the scholarship process. Application Deadline: May 29, 2026 Winner Notification Date: On or before June 30, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their materials well before the deadline to avoid last-minute issues. Why Students Should Apply Law school can be financially demanding, and scholarships like this provide critical support that allows students to focus more effectively on their studies and career aspirations. Beyond the monetary award, receiving recognition from an established law firm can significantly strengthen a student’s academic and professional profile. It also demonstrates a student’s commitment to leadership, excellence, and community service; qualities highly valued within the legal profession. Students who are passionate about law, advocacy, and creating positive societal change should strongly consider applying for this opportunity. How to Apply Eligible students must complete the official online application provided by The Barnes Firm. Applicants should ensure all required materials are submitted before the deadline. Final Thoughts The Barnes Firm Law School Scholarship 2026 presents an excellent opportunity for aspiring legal professionals in the United States to receive financial assistance while gaining recognition for their academic and community achievements. As the legal field continues to evolve, initiatives like this play an essential role in empowering future lawyers who are committed to leadership, advocacy, and service. Students who meet the eligibility requirements are encouraged to apply early and submit a strong application that highlights both their academic accomplishments and their dedication to making a positive impact in society. Deadline : 29th May,2026 APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here For more global opportunities, click here The post The Barnes Firm Law School Scholarship 2026 in the USA | $5,000 Tuition Support for Law Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Call for Partners for the 25th University on Youth and Development 2026 in Spain: Focus on AI, Youth, and Peace
summary: The 25th edition of the University on Youth and Development (UYD) is officially calling for partners interested in shaping one of the most impactful international youth events focused on artificial intelligence, youth engagement, peacebuilding, and responsible digital governance. Scheduled to take place in Mollina, Spain, from 13 to 20 September 2026, this landmark edition invites youth-led and youth-oriented organisations from across Europe and beyond to organise activities aligned with the 2026 annual theme: “AI, Youth, and Peace: Preventing cybercrime and digital harm through responsible AI governance.” Organised by the North-South Centre of the Council of Europe within the framework of the iLEGEND III project, the University on Youth and Development has become a globally recognised platform for youth cooperation, dialogue, advocacy, networking, and leadership development. The 2026 edition aims to empower young people and organisations to contribute meaningfully to conversations surrounding ethical artificial intelligence, cyber safety, digital peace, and responsible innovation. Applications for activity proposals are currently open, and interested organisations must submit their proposals before 24 May 2026. What is the University on Youth and Development (UYD)? The University on Youth and Development is an international youth event that has been bringing together youth organisations, institutions, decision-makers, field experts, and young leaders since the year 2000. Over the years, the UYD has evolved into a powerful international hub for youth advocacy, intercultural dialogue, peer learning, and collaborative action. Held annually in Mollina, Spain, the programme creates a unique environment where organisations can host their own trainings, seminars, workshops, board meetings, discussions, and capacity-building activities within a broader collaborative framework. The event attracts hundreds of participants from around the world who engage in week-long activities focused on youth policies, social development, leadership, peacebuilding, democracy, and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The 2026 edition places a strong emphasis on artificial intelligence and its impact on youth, society, and peace. 2026 Theme: AI, Youth, and Peace This year’s annual theme, “AI, Youth, and Peace: Preventing cybercrime and digital harm through responsible AI governance,” addresses one of the most important global conversations of our time. As artificial intelligence continues to reshape education, communication, employment, governance, and social interaction, there is an increasing need to ensure that young people possess the skills and critical understanding necessary to navigate AI-powered digital environments safely and responsibly. The 2026 University on Youth and Development will explore several major questions, including: How can young people develop critical AI literacy? What skills are necessary to navigate AI-driven digital spaces? How can youth contribute to ethical AI governance? What role can young people play in preventing cybercrime and digital harm? How can international cooperation strengthen digital peacebuilding initiatives? The programme aims to encourage meaningful youth participation in discussions around digital rights, cyber ethics, online safety, and peace-oriented technology governance. Why Organisations Should Participate Participating organisations will gain the opportunity to become official partners of the 25th edition of the UYD and collaborate with an international network of youth organisations, institutions, and policy stakeholders. Benefits of participation include: International Visibility Selected organisations will showcase their work on an international stage alongside leading youth institutions and organisations from around the world. Networking Opportunities The event provides extensive opportunities to connect with youth leaders, policy experts, NGOs, institutions, and development partners. Capacity Building Participants can engage in high-level discussions, workshops, peer-learning activities, and collaborative projects focused on AI governance, peacebuilding, and youth empowerment. Collaboration and Partnerships Organisations joining the UYD partnership will collaborate with numerous international actors to strengthen the youth sector and develop sustainable initiatives. Contribution to Global Youth Policy Partners will actively contribute to conversations aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals and international youth policy priorities. Promotion of Youth Empowerment The event supports organisations committed to empowering young people as agents of positive social change. What Types of Activities Can Be Proposed? Organisations are invited to submit activity proposals connected to the annual theme. Proposed activities may include: Trainings Workshops Seminars Board meetings Roundtable discussions Capacity-building sessions Networking events Policy dialogues Educational programmes Peer-learning activities Activities should align with the broader themes of AI, digital governance, cyber safety, youth participation, peacebuilding, and ethical technology development. How to Apply Interested organisations are encouraged to carefully review all official documents before submitting their proposals. Applicants should read the annual theme concept note and the official call for activities to fully understand the commitments, expectations, and partnership framework involved in participating in the event. Required documents include: Annual Theme Concept Note Call for Activities Activity Proposal Form Activity proposals must be submitted no later than 24 May 2026. Selected organisations will officially join the partnership framework for the 25th edition of the University on Youth and Development and contribute to the successful implementation of the event. About the Organisers The University on Youth and Development is organised within the framework of the iLEGEND III project, co-funded by the European Union and the Council of Europe and implemented by the North-South Centre of the Council of Europe. The initiative reflects a long-standing commitment to strengthening global youth participation, intercultural dialogue, democratic engagement, and sustainable development through international cooperation. For organisations passionate about youth empowerment, peacebuilding, ethical technology, and responsible AI governance, the 25th edition of the UYD presents a major opportunity to engage with global stakeholders and help shape the future of youth participation in the digital age. Deadline: 24th May,2026. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. title: Heritage and Displacement PhD Scholarship 2026 in Australia | Fully Funded Research Opportunity at Deakin University
summary: The global conversation around displacement, exile, conflict, and cultural identity continues to shape international policy, humanitarian action, and academic inquiry. In response to these pressing global issues, Deakin University is offering an exceptional fully funded PhD scholarship opportunity for outstanding research candidates interested in exploring the intersections of heritage, conflict, migration, and displacement. Applications are now open for the “Heritage and Displacement: Intersections of Exile, Culture and Conflict” PhD Scholarship project supervised by Professor Benjamin Isakhan at the School of Humanities and Social Sciences, Faculty of Arts and Education, located at the university’s Burwood Campus in Australia. This prestigious scholarship forms part of a broader Australian Research Council Future Fellowship project and aims to generate groundbreaking research on how displaced communities understand, preserve, and relate to their cultural heritage amidst experiences of conflict, migration, and exile. Applications close on 30 June 2026. About the Heritage and Displacement Research Project The project titled “Heritage and Displacement: Intersections of Exile, Culture and Conflict” investigates the complex relationship between heritage and displacement among people affected by war, conflict, forced migration, and exile. The scholarship seeks to support two PhD candidates who will contribute to an interdisciplinary research initiative examining the lived experiences of displaced populations from Syria, Iraq, and Palestine. The research project focuses on understanding how displaced individuals perceive their heritage and cultural identity, and how those perspectives align or conflict with the approaches adopted by governments, humanitarian organizations, and international agencies. The project aims to produce innovative scholarly insights through: Development of new conceptual frameworks Field-based qualitative research Interviews with displaced communities Comparative analysis across multiple regions and migration contexts The findings are expected to significantly influence future academic research, international heritage policies, and global humanitarian responses relating to displacement and cultural preservation. Research Focus and Case Studies Selected PhD scholars will undertake independent research projects connected to broader themes of heritage and displacement. The case studies will focus on displaced populations from: Syria Iraq Palestine Research participants may include: Internally displaced persons Refugees Migrants The research locations extend across: Jordan Lebanon Germany United Kingdom Australia Candidates are expected to design strong research proposals that include a fieldwork component and demonstrate methodological feasibility. This is a unique opportunity for emerging scholars interested in migration, cultural identity, conflict studies, and Middle Eastern politics to contribute to a globally significant academic initiative. Scholarship Benefits The Heritage and Displacement PhD Scholarship provides substantial financial and academic support for successful candidates. Scholarship Funding Includes: Annual tax-free stipend of AUD $37,450 (2026 rate) Scholarship duration of three years Relocation allowance ranging from AUD $500 to AUD $1,500 for students moving interstate within Australia Additional Benefits for International Students: Full tuition fee offset for up to four years Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC) for the duration of the student visa Beyond financial support, scholars will also gain: Mentorship from leading academic experts Opportunities to co-author high-impact scholarly publications Participation in policy reports and media publications Invitations to project workshops and academic presentations Significant career development opportunities within an internationally relevant research project Why This Scholarship Matters Displacement and cultural heritage preservation remain critical issues in today’s world. Millions of people globally have experienced forced migration due to conflict, war, persecution, and instability. This scholarship directly contributes to understanding: How displaced communities preserve cultural memory The role of heritage in identity formation during exile The impact of displacement on social and cultural belonging Policy gaps in global responses to refugee heritage and identity The project’s interdisciplinary nature makes it highly valuable for students interested in: International relations Migration studies Anthropology Sociology Political science Heritage studies Human rights research Middle Eastern studies Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the scholarship, applicants must: Meet Deakin University’s PhD admission requirements Be available to enrol as a full-time PhD student Hold a first-class honours degree or equivalent master’s qualification with significant research training Applicants must also demonstrate: Excellent English communication skills Strong academic writing abilities Research competence and analytical capacity Although not compulsory, preference may be given to applicants with: Research experience related to heritage or political science in the Middle East Qualitative research experience Experience conducting interviews and fieldwork Academic publishing experience Application Process Interested candidates are required to submit the following documents directly to Professor Benjamin Isakhan: Updated CV Academic transcripts and grades Relevant work experience details Research publications (if available) Cover letter addressing eligibility criteria and research interests PhD research proposal (maximum 2,000 words) The research proposal should include: Project title Introduction Research questions Literature review Methodology Fieldwork strategy Feasibility considerations References Applicants are encouraged to develop innovative and well-structured proposals aligned with the project’s broader themes. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Heritage and Displacement PhD Scholarship is: 30 June 2026 Interested applicants should begin preparing their application materials early to ensure sufficient time for proposal development and document review. Final Thoughts The Heritage and Displacement PhD Scholarship at Deakin University presents a remarkable opportunity for aspiring researchers passionate about migration, conflict, heritage, and global humanitarian issues. With full financial support, international research exposure, mentorship opportunities, and participation in a globally impactful project, this scholarship stands out as one of the most meaningful humanities and social science PhD opportunities available in 2026. For students and researchers seeking to make a lasting contribution to understanding displacement and cultural identity in conflict-affected societies, this opportunity offers both academic advancement and real-world impact. Deadline : 30th June,2026. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. title: 5 Free World Bank Online Courses in Data, Research, Agriculture, and Development Practice for 2026
summary: The World Bank Group is offering a collection of free online professional courses designed to help learners worldwide strengthen practical skills in data visualization, field research, policy development, agriculture, and gender statistics. These self-paced and virtual learning opportunities are ideal for students, researchers, development professionals, policymakers, NGO workers, and anyone seeking to build high-demand skills in evidence-based decision-making and sustainable development practice. The courses combine practical learning, real-world applications, and professional development tools to help participants improve their expertise while learning from globally recognized development practitioners and specialists. Several courses also provide free certificates upon successful completion. Why These Free World Bank Courses Matter In today’s rapidly evolving global economy, professionals increasingly need skills in: Data analysis and visualization Research implementation Evidence-based policymaking Sustainable agriculture systems Gender-responsive development planning Technology-enabled data systems The World Bank Group’s online learning platform helps bridge knowledge gaps by making high-quality training accessible globally at no cost. These courses are particularly valuable for professionals working in: International development Public policy Agriculture and food systems Monitoring and evaluation Humanitarian response Education and research Gender and social inclusion Climate and sustainability sectors 1. Fundamentals of Data Visualization: Crafting Compelling Stories with Data Course Overview This course introduces learners to the fundamentals of data visualization and its importance in effective communication and decision-making. Participants learn how to transform raw data into clear, engaging, and actionable visual stories. The training focuses on helping learners choose the right visual format for different datasets and communication objectives. What Participants Will Learn Key topics include: Choosing the best chart type for different datasets Understanding chart anatomy and visual design principles Creating intuitive and impactful visualizations Designing maps and infographics using Excel Simplifying complex information into understandable visuals Applying best practices in visual storytelling Course Details Duration: 8 hours Training Type: e-Learning Videos: 9 instructional videos Certificate: Free completion certificate Enrollment Information Enrollment is currently open. Course content becomes available from: March 31, 2026 This course is highly recommended for: Data analysts Researchers Students Communications professionals Monitoring and evaluation specialists Development practitioners Click here for more and to apply 2. Manage Successful Field Research: New Edition – Spring 2026 Course Overview This virtual course is designed for professionals responsible for planning, managing, and implementing field research projects. It provides practical guidance on handling real-world research challenges while improving operational effectiveness and data quality. The course introduces participants to best practices and emerging technologies used in modern field research environments. What Participants Will Learn Participants will explore: Research implementation best practices Common fieldwork challenges and solutions Research ethics and operational management Data collection systems and technologies Team coordination and field supervision Quality assurance techniques Who Should Apply Ideal participants include: Researchers NGO professionals Survey coordinators Monitoring and evaluation officers Development consultants Academic practitioners Click here for more and to apply 3. Foundations in Mobile Phone Data (MPD) for Policy Course Overview This non-technical practitioner course introduces learners to the use of mobile phone metadata for policymaking and development planning. The programme helps newcomers understand both the opportunities and limitations of mobile phone data in public policy and social impact initiatives. What Participants Will Learn The course covers: What mobile phone data can and cannot achieve Real-world policy applications of mobile data Assessing available data and institutional capacity Planning and launching data-driven initiatives Ethical considerations in data use Practical implementation frameworks Why This Course Is Important Mobile phone data is increasingly being used to: Track population movement Support disaster response Improve public service delivery Inform transportation planning Strengthen development interventions This course helps professionals understand how to responsibly use such data for evidence-based decision-making. Click here for more and to apply 4. Designing Scalable Evidence-Based Solutions for Better Jobs in Agriculture Course Overview This masterclass is part of the AgriConnect learning ecosystem developed under the World Bank Group’s LEADS Framework (Learn, Adapt, Scale). The programme focuses on improving agricultural systems and agribusiness development using evidence-based interventions. Key Focus Areas Participants learn how to design scalable agricultural solutions across: Agricultural extension services Irrigation systems Input supply systems Climate resilience interventions Market access initiatives Agribusiness development What Makes This Masterclass Valuable The course helps professionals understand how to: Develop inclusive agricultural interventions Build cost-effective farming systems Strengthen value chains Improve farmer livelihoods Support sustainable agribusiness growth Course Details Training Type: Masterclass Duration: 34 minutes This is ideal for: Agricultural practitioners Policymakers Rural development professionals Agribusiness experts Climate adaptation specialists Click here for more and to apply 5. From Data to Impact: Strengthening Gender Statistics Across the Data Lifecycle Course Overview This course provides a comprehensive introduction to gender data and its role in improving policies, programming, and investment decisions. Participants learn how to identify gender data gaps and transform gender statistics into actionable insights. What Participants Will Learn The course explores: Gender data collection methods Gender statistics production and analysis Data dissemination strategies Using gender data for policymaking Addressing data gaps across the data lifecycle Evidence-based gender programming Why Gender Data Matters Reliable gender statistics are essential for: Expanding women’s economic opportunities Improving inclusive policymaking Designing equitable development programmes Supporting informed investment decisions The course combines technical knowledge with practical application strategies. Click here for more and to apply Benefits of These Free World Bank Courses Participants can benefit from: Flexible self-paced online learning Free access to expert-led training Practical development-focused skills Professional certificates (for selected courses) Global learning opportunities Real-world case studies and applications Career advancement in development and policy sectors Who Should Take These Courses? These programmes are suitable for individuals interested in: Data science and analytics International development Public policy Sustainable agriculture Gender equality Research management Climate and sustainability work Monitoring and evaluation Whether you are a student, early-career professional, or experienced practitioner, these courses provide valuable practical knowledge that can strengthen your career and professional impact. Conclusion The World Bank Group continues to expand access to high-quality professional education through free online learning opportunities focused on global development challenges. These five courses provide learners with practical, career-relevant skills in data visualization, research implementation, agriculture, policy innovation, and gender-responsive development. For professionals looking to strengthen their expertise while learning at their own pace, these programmes offer an excellent opportunity to gain globally relevant knowledge from one of the world’s leading development institutions.
